title: Contact
text:
I welcome discussions on potential collaborative research projects, including academic partnerships and research collaborations with public institutions or industry.
For an overview of my current research interests and agenda, please see the Research page.
If you are considering collaboration, a short description of your research question and objectives would be helpful.

title: Contact
text:
Students and Early-Career Researchers
I am happy to hear from motivated students and postdoctoral researchers whose interests align with the areas above.
When reaching out, please include:

A CV (PDF)
A brief statement (1–2 paragraphs) outlining your interests and research goals
(Optional) a writing sample or code repository


Possible opportunities

MSc thesis supervision

PhD positions (subject to availability and funding)

Visiting research stays

Fellowship-based positions (see below)

title: Contact
text:
Fellowship Opportunities
Prospective applicants are encouraged to consider external funding schemes. Examples include:

JSPS Postdoctoral Fellowship (PD)
https://www.jsps.go.jp/english/e-pd/
JSPS Overseas Research Fellowship (海外特別研究員)
https://www.jsps.go.jp/english/e-ab/
Overseas fellowships (non-Japanese applicants to Japan)
I can host applicants at Nagasaki University under relevant fellowship schemes.
(For example: JSPS Invitational / institutional programs depending on year.)
Marie Skłodowska-Curie Postdoctoral Fellowships (MSCA PF)
https://marie-sklodowska-curie-actions.ec.europa.eu/actions/postdoctoral-fellowships

If you are considering applying for a fellowship with me as host or collaborator, please contact me well in advance of the deadline.

title: Contact
text:
Working Style
I value:

clarity of research questions

steady, structured writing from an early stage

reproducible workflows (version control, well-documented code, organized projects)

constructive and open communication

I aim to provide an environment that supports intellectual independence while maintaining high analytical standards.

title: About
text:
I am a researcher working at the intersection of infectious disease modelling and theoretical epidemiology.
My work focuses on understanding how transmission processes unfold across heterogeneous populations and how mathematical theory can inform practical control strategies.
I am particularly interested in:

structural properties of transmission systems

network and multi-layer epidemic models

time-varying dominance of transmission routes

reduced-order representations of complex epidemic dynamics

I aim to bridge epidemic theory and real-world decision making through parsimonious, interpretable models. You can find my CV here.

title: Writing
text:
Start with a clear research question and scope
Write early; revise often (small iterations)
Prefer short, structured drafts (bullets → paragraphs)
